data.name: Accounting logic follows a 'Pool Model': money flows from one pool (source) to another pool (destination). Understanding these five elements is key to double-entry bookkeeping.
data.value: Accounting logic follows a 'Pool Model': money flows from one pool (source) to another pool (destination). Understanding these five elements is key to double-entry bookkeeping.

data.name: Bank deposits, Accounts Receivable (money others owe you), Servers/Laptops.
data.value: Bank deposits, Accounts Receivable (money others owe you), Servers/Laptops.

data.name: Borrowing money = Increase; Repaying money = Decrease.
data.value: Borrowing money = Increase; Repaying money = Decrease.

data.name: Cash on hand, or things that can be converted into cash.
data.value: Cash on hand, or assets that can be converted into cash.

data.name: Debts that the company must repay in the future.
data.value: Debts that the company must repay in the future.

data.name: Director loans (money you lent to the company), Unpaid invoices, Credit card debt.
data.value: Director loans (money you lent to the company), Unpaid invoices, Credit card debt.

data.name: Money earned by selling products or providing services. Increases profit.
data.value: Money earned by selling products or providing services. Increases profit.

data.name: Money flows from bank to eliminate debt
data.value: Money flows from bank to eliminate debt

data.name: Money flows from bank to expenses
data.value: Money flows from bank to expenses

data.name: Money flows from debt to bank account
data.value: Money flows from debt to bank account

data.name: Money flows from equity to bank account
data.value: Money flows from equity to bank account

data.name: Money generated as income into bank
data.value: Money generated as income into bank

data.name: Money in = Increase; Money out = Decrease.
data.value: Money in = Increase; Money out = Decrease.

data.name: Money spent to maintain company operations. Decreases profit.
data.value: Money spent to maintain company operations. Decreases profit.

data.name: Sales Revenue, Service Fees, Interest Income.
data.value: Sales Revenue, Service Fees, Interest Income.

data.name: Server hosting fees, Bank charges, Salaries.
data.value: Server hosting fees, Bank charges, Salaries.

data.name: Share Capital (initial investment), Retained Earnings (accumulated profit).
data.value: Share Capital (initial investment), Retained Earnings (accumulated profit).

data.name: Shareholder investment/Profit = Increase; Shareholder withdrawal/Loss = Decrease.
data.value: Shareholder investment/Profit = Increase; Shareholder withdrawal/Loss = Decrease.

data.name: The net worth of the company. Formula: Asset - Liability = Equity.
data.value: The net worth of the company. Formula: Asset - Liability = Equity.

data.name: Value that truly belongs to you
data.value: Value that truly belongs to you
